channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Blue Economy Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded UBC Opportunity for East African Professionals (Apply Before April 30)
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD), in partnership with the globally renowned University of British Columbia (UBC), has launched an exciting opportunity for professionals in East Africa through the Blue Grant Scholarship under the BE-Prosper Project. This highly competitive and impactful scholarship is designed to build regional expertise in the blue economy, focusing on sustainable […]

The post Blue Economy Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded UBC Opportunity for East African Professionals (Apply Before April 30) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD), in partnership with the globally renowned University of British Columbia (UBC), has launched an exciting opportunity for professionals in East Africa through the Blue Grant Scholarship under the BE-Prosper Project.

This highly competitive and impactful scholarship is designed to build regional expertise in the blue economy, focusing on sustainable ocean management, fisheries, and marine governance across the IGAD and Horn of Africa region.

If you are passionate about marine resources, sustainability, and regional development, this opportunity could shape your academic and leadership journey at one of the world’s top universities.

Application Deadline

The deadline to submit your screening application is April 30, 2026.

About the Blue Grant Scholarship (BE-Prosper Project)

The Blue Grant Scholarship is part of the Building Blue Economy Resilience and Prosperity in IGAD & Horn of Africa (BE-Prosper) initiative. This program aims to:

  • Strengthen regional capacity in blue economy leadership
  • Enhance knowledge in fisheries value chains and management
  • Improve coastal governance and marine spatial planning
  • Promote sustainable ocean and marine resource management

This initiative is especially critical for countries in the Horn of Africa, where marine and coastal resources are key drivers of economic growth and environmental sustainability.

Why Study at the University of British Columbia (UBC)?

The University of British Columbia is globally recognized for excellence in:

  • Fisheries economics and management
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Marine and ocean sciences

As a scholar, you will gain:

  • Access to world-class research facilities
  • Opportunities to collaborate with leading global experts
  • Exposure to cutting-edge solutions in blue economy development

Program Structure: Two-Phase Application Process

The application process is carefully structured to ensure the selection of the most qualified candidates.

Phase 1: IGAD Screening

Applicants must first undergo a screening process conducted by IGAD.

Phase 2: UBC Graduate Admission

Successful candidates from the IGAD screening will proceed to apply for admission to UBC, where final selection and scholarship placement occur.

IGAD Screening Criteria

To be considered, applicants should meet the following criteria:

  1. Interest in Blue Economy
    • Demonstrated academic or professional interest in:
      • Fisheries management
      • Fish value chains
      • Maritime connectivity and trade corridors
      • Coastal and ocean resource management
  2. Educational Background
    • A university degree in relevant disciplines such as:
      • Marine and fisheries science
      • Environmental economics
      • Agriculture economics
      • Maritime or civil engineering
      • Human geography
      • Veterinary science
      • Community development
      • Ocean policy or related fields
  3. Age Requirement
    • Applicants must be between 25 and 40 years old
  4. Professional Experience
    • Must be currently working in:
      • Government or public sector
      • Private sector
      • NGOs or civil society organizations
      • International or intergovernmental institutions
  5. Regional Eligibility
    • Must be from:
      • South Sudan
      • Somalia
      • Djibouti
  6. Motivation and Impact
    • Strong commitment to:
      • Sustainable fisheries
      • Responsible marine resource use
      • Blue economy development in the region
  7. Collaboration Interest
    • Willingness to:
      • Work with IGAD and UBC experts
      • Contribute to solving regional challenges

How to Apply for IGAD Screening

Applicants are required to submit the following documents:

  • Letter of Interest (LoI) addressing all screening criteria
  • Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Copy of university degree

Submission Details

Send your application via email to:

  • sagal.isman@igad.int
  • Copy: tegueste.shimelis@igad.int 

For inquiries, contact:

  • eshete.dejen@igad.int

UBC Graduate Application Requirements

Candidates who pass the IGAD screening will proceed to the next stage at UBC.

The graduate application typically includes:

  1. Completed application form with fees
  2. Reference letters
  3. Statement of interest
  4. English language test (if applicable)
  5. Criminal record check
  6. Identification of a thesis supervisor
  7. Proof of citizenship

For full application details, visit the UBC graduate admissions portal.

Key Benefits of the Blue Grant Scholarship

Although specific funding details are not explicitly outlined, the scholarship is described as fully funded, implying support for:

  • Tuition fees
  • Academic training
  • Research opportunities
  • International exposure

Additionally, scholars benefit from:

  • Participation in a high-impact regional initiative
  • Development of leadership skills in blue economy sectors
  • Contribution to sustainable development in East Africa

Why This Opportunity Matters

The blue economy represents one of the fastest-growing sectors globally, with immense potential for:

  • Job creation
  • Food security
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Regional trade

Through this scholarship, IGAD and UBC aim to build a network of skilled professionals who will:

  • Drive innovation in marine industries
  • Influence policy and governance
  • Strengthen regional cooperation

Click HERE to learn more about the Blue Grant Scholarship

Final Tips for Applicants

To stand out in your application:

  • Clearly align your experience with blue economy priorities
  • Demonstrate real-world impact in your work
  • Show strong motivation for regional development
  • Be specific about how the program will help you achieve your goals

Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE

The post Blue Economy Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded UBC Opportunity for East African Professionals (Apply Before April 30) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: JEP 2026 – Call for Applications
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for JEP 2026, a program designed for scholars advancing research in education policy with a focus on racial justice. About the Program JEP (Junior Education Policy) brings together emerging researchers in a collaborative learning environment to strengthen their research agendas and impact. Participants will: Engage in community-based learning with peersRefine and […]

The post JEP 2026 – Call for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for JEP 2026, a program designed for scholars advancing research in education policy with a focus on racial justice.

About the Program

JEP (Junior Education Policy) brings together emerging researchers in a collaborative learning environment to strengthen their research agendas and impact.

Participants will:

Engage in community-based learning with peers
Refine and develop their research focus
Receive mentorship and guidance from experienced scholars
Explore how research can drive equity and systemic change

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is ideal for:

Current doctoral students in education policy
Postdoctoral researchers working on education and racial justice
Scholars interested in equity-focused research and policy impact

Applicants should demonstrate:

A strong interest in racial justice in education
Commitment to advancing impactful research
Willingness to engage in collaborative learning

Key Details

Program: JEP 2026
Eligibility: Doctoral students and post-docs in education policy
Focus Area: Racial justice in education
Application Deadline: June 1, 2026

Why This Opportunity Matters

JEP provides a supportive space for emerging scholars to build meaningful, equity-driven research while connecting with a community committed to transforming education systems.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For More Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post JEP 2026 – Call for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Internship Opportunity – Optimus TPM Team
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Optimus TPM Team is hiring an intern for Summer and Fall 2026. This is a great opportunity to gain hands-on experience working on cutting-edge technology projects within a dynamic team environment. About the Role As an intern, you will: Support technology and program management initiativesCollaborate with cross-functional teamsContribute to innovative and impactful tech projectsGain […]

The post Internship Opportunity – Optimus TPM Team appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Optimus TPM Team is hiring an intern for Summer and Fall 2026.

This is a great opportunity to gain hands-on experience working on cutting-edge technology projects within a dynamic team environment.

About the Role

As an intern, you will:

Support technology and program management initiatives
Collaborate with cross-functional teams
Contribute to innovative and impactful tech projects
Gain exposure to real-world product and system development

Who Should Apply

This role is ideal for individuals who:

Are students or recent graduates in tech-related fields
Have an interest in program management, product development, or engineering
Are eager to learn and work on innovative technology
Can thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment

Key Details

Role: Intern (TPM – Technology Program Management)
Duration: Summer and Fall 2026
Application: Rolling (apply early)

Why This Opportunity Matters

This internship offers valuable exposure to the tech industry, helping you build practical skills, expand your network, and gain experience working on high-impact projects.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Internship Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Internship Opportunity – Optimus TPM Team appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SRHR Ambassadors 2026 Cohort – Call for Applications
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Jonica Foundation is recruiting County SRHR Ambassadors for its 2026 cohort. This opportunity is designed for young women who are ready to lead conversations and drive change in sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) within their communities. About the Program The SRHR Ambassadors initiative empowers young women to become advocates and educators on […]

The post SRHR Ambassadors 2026 Cohort – Call for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Jonica Foundation is recruiting County SRHR Ambassadors for its 2026 cohort.

This opportunity is designed for young women who are ready to lead conversations and drive change in sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) within their communities.

About the Program

The SRHR Ambassadors initiative empowers young women to become advocates and educators on critical health and social issues affecting women and girls.

Ambassadors will:

Lead community conversations on SRHR topics
Raise awareness and educate peers
Advocate for better health outcomes
Engage in leadership and personal development

Focus Areas

Participants will work on key topics such as:

Menstrual health
Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)
Gender-Based Violence (GBV)
Contraception awareness
Menopause education

Who Should Apply

This program is open to:

Young women in universities and colleges
Residents of Nairobi, Kiambu, Narok, Homa Bay, and Kilifi (Kenya)
Individuals passionate about advocacy, health education, and community impact

Key Details

Program: SRHR Ambassadors 2026 Cohort
Organization: Jonica Foundation
Location: Kenya (selected counties)
Application Deadline: 15 May 2026

Why This Opportunity Matters

SRHR issues directly affect the well-being, rights, and opportunities of women and girls. This program equips young women with the knowledge, voice, and leadership skills to drive meaningful change in their communities.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Leadership opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post SRHR Ambassadors 2026 Cohort – Call for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Move to Estonia Without IELTS 2026/2027 – Scholarships
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Looking to study or work in Europe without IELTS? Estonia offers a range of scholarships and grants for international students, researchers, and academic staff. These opportunities are coordinated by the Estonian Ministry of Education and Research and the Education and Youth Board (Harno), aiming to attract global talent and promote academic collaboration. About the Opportunity […]

The post Move to Estonia Without IELTS 2026/2027 – Scholarships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Looking to study or work in Europe without IELTS? Estonia offers a range of scholarships and grants for international students, researchers, and academic staff.

These opportunities are coordinated by the Estonian Ministry of Education and Research and the Education and Youth Board (Harno), aiming to attract global talent and promote academic collaboration.

About the Opportunity

Estonia provides multiple funding options under the Estonian Scholarship Programme, supporting:

Degree studies (Bachelor’s, Master’s, PhD)
Short-term exchange programs
Summer and winter schools
Research and academic collaboration

These programs are designed to strengthen international cooperation and support innovation in education and research.

Can You Study Without IELTS?

In many cases, yes.

Some Estonian universities allow alternatives such as:

Proof that your previous education was in English
Online interviews or internal language assessments

However, requirements vary by university and program, so always confirm before applying.

Types of Scholarships Available

1. Government Scholarships

Funded by Estonia to support international students across different levels of study.

2. Scholarships for Students

Support for degree and exchange studies, especially at Master’s and PhD levels.

3. Grants for Researchers & Academic Staff

For teaching, research collaboration, and academic exchange at Estonian universities.

4. Summer & Short-Term Programmes

Funding to attend short courses and academic programs in Estonia.

What the Funding Covers

Depending on the program, support may include:

Tuition fees (full or partial)
Monthly stipend
Travel or accommodation support
Participation fees for courses

Who Can Apply

Eligible applicants include:

International students (Bachelor’s, Master’s, PhD)
Researchers and academic staff
Individuals interested in short-term academic programs

Key Details

Location: Estonia
Eligible Levels: Bachelor’s, Master’s, PhD, Research
Application Deadline: Around May 2026 (varies by program; some close May 14, 2026)

Why This Opportunity Matters

Estonia is known for its strong digital economy and innovative education system. These scholarships offer a pathway to study, research, and build international experience in Europe, often with flexible language requirements.

How to Apply

Learn more and apply here.

For more Scholarship opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Move to Estonia Without IELTS 2026/2027 – Scholarships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NASA Nebraska Space Grant Fellowship & Mini-Grants 2026: Funding for STEM Research and Workforce Development
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The NASA Nebraska Space Grant Fellowship & Mini-Grants Competition 2026 offers a valuable opportunity for students, researchers, and higher education institutions to advance their work in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) while contributing to the future aerospace workforce. Designed to support academic achievement, research innovation, and workforce development, this program provides funding and professional […]

The post NASA Nebraska Space Grant Fellowship & Mini-Grants 2026: Funding for STEM Research and Workforce Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The NASA Nebraska Space Grant Fellowship & Mini-Grants Competition 2026 offers a valuable opportunity for students, researchers, and higher education institutions to advance their work in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) while contributing to the future aerospace workforce. Designed to support academic achievement, research innovation, and workforce development, this program provides funding and professional development pathways for individuals committed to STEM careers.

This opportunity is particularly suited for students with a strong interest in STEM fields, faculty researchers engaged in aerospace-related studies, and institutions seeking to strengthen educational programs that prepare the next generation of scientists and engineers.

About the Program

The NASA Nebraska Space Grant is part of a national network aimed at building research capacity and developing talent in aerospace and related fields. The 2026 competition includes three main funding streams: student fellowships, research mini-grants, and higher education mini-grants.

Student fellowships are awarded to individuals who demonstrate academic excellence and a clear commitment to STEM careers. Unlike traditional scholarships, these fellowships require applicants to design and propose a project, such as a research initiative, internship, or workforce development activity. This structure ensures that participants gain practical, hands-on experience while contributing to real-world challenges.

Research mini-grants are targeted at faculty members in Nebraska who are conducting aerospace-related research. These grants aim to strengthen collaboration with NASA scientists and enhance the state’s research infrastructure in fields aligned with NASA’s mission.

Higher education mini-grants focus on institutional innovation. They support projects and programs that encourage students—particularly those from underrepresented groups—to pursue careers in aerospace and STEM. These may include curriculum development, design competitions, and other initiatives that foster engagement and skill-building.

Why This Opportunity Matters

As the global demand for STEM professionals continues to grow, initiatives like the NASA Nebraska Space Grant play a critical role in preparing a skilled and diverse workforce. By combining funding with experiential learning, the program bridges the gap between academic knowledge and real-world application.

It also strengthens regional research ecosystems by supporting faculty and institutions, ensuring that innovation is not limited to major research hubs. For students, the opportunity to design and implement their own projects fosters independence, creativity, and problem-solving—skills that are highly valued in today’s workforce.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in this program benefit from more than just financial support. The fellowship and grant structure emphasizes experiential learning, allowing individuals to develop practical skills through research, internships, or project-based work.

Students gain early exposure to professional environments and build portfolios that demonstrate their capabilities. Faculty researchers benefit from enhanced collaboration opportunities and resources to advance their work. Institutions, in turn, can strengthen their academic offerings and better support student pathways into STEM careers.

Overall, the program helps participants build technical expertise, expand professional networks, and position themselves for future opportunities in aerospace and related industries.

Who Can Apply

Eligibility varies depending on the funding stream. Student fellowships are open to individuals with strong academic performance and a demonstrated interest in STEM careers. Applicants must be prepared to propose and carry out a project aligned with workforce development goals.

Research mini-grants are available to faculty members based in Nebraska who are engaged in aerospace-related research. Higher education mini-grants are designed for colleges and universities seeking to develop or expand programs that support student engagement in STEM fields.

Eligible Areas

This opportunity is specifically targeted at participants who are U.S. citizens.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants are required to submit their proposals through the official NASA Nebraska Space Grant platform. Student applicants must outline a clear project plan, while faculty and institutions must provide detailed proposals aligned with program objectives.

The application deadline for the 2026 competition is May 20, 2026. Interested applicants are encouraged to begin preparing early, ensuring that their proposals clearly demonstrate impact, feasibility, and alignment with NASA’s mission.

Learn more and apply on the NE Space Grant website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post NASA Nebraska Space Grant Fellowship & Mini-Grants 2026: Funding for STEM Research and Workforce Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Schwarzman Scholars 2026 Application Guide: Expert Tips to Build a Winning Leadership Application Before the September Deadline
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Schwarzman Scholars Program stands among the world’s most prestigious global leadership programs, bringing together exceptional young leaders from across the globe to study in China and engage deeply with global affairs. As the application cycle progresses, the admissions team has released a comprehensive set of insights and strategies to help applicants craft strong, compelling, […]

The post Schwarzman Scholars 2026 Application Guide: Expert Tips to Build a Winning Leadership Application Before the September Deadline appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Schwarzman Scholars Program stands among the world’s most prestigious global leadership programs, bringing together exceptional young leaders from across the globe to study in China and engage deeply with global affairs. As the application cycle progresses, the admissions team has released a comprehensive set of insights and strategies to help applicants craft strong, compelling, and competitive submissions.

Applying to Schwarzman Scholars requires more than academic excellence. It demands authenticity, clarity of purpose, and a demonstrated track record of leadership. With the application deadline set for September 9 at 3:00 PM EDT, applicants are encouraged to begin early, plan strategically, and refine every aspect of their application.

This guide compiles the official application tips, frequently asked questions, and expert advice provided by the Schwarzman Scholars Admissions Team to help candidates present their best selves.

Overview of the Schwarzman Scholars Application Process

The Schwarzman Scholars application is designed to assess candidates holistically, focusing on:

  • Leadership potential
  • Academic excellence
  • Character and integrity
  • Commitment to global impact

Applicants are required to submit multiple components that together tell a cohesive leadership story.

Key Application Deadline

  • Final Deadline: September 9 at 3:00 PM EDT

Applicants must ensure that all materials, including recommendation letters, are submitted before this deadline, as late submissions are not accepted.

Essential Components of the Application

1. Resume

Your resume should differ significantly from a traditional job application resume.

Key recommendations:

  • Focus on leadership experiences rather than just job roles
  • Include:
    • Club affiliations
    • Internships
    • Volunteer experiences
    • Hobbies that demonstrate leadership or initiative
  • Use the resume as a supporting document for your leadership narrative

2. Essays (Leadership & Statement of Purpose)

The essays are central to your application and should:

  • Tell a cohesive personal story
  • Include personal anecdotes that demonstrate leadership
  • Highlight:
    • Problem-solving skills
    • Impact created
    • Vision for the future

Important guidance:

  • Avoid over-describing settings; focus on impact and outcomes
  • Use authentic voice and tone
  • Choose topics that genuinely excite and reflect your personality

3. Letters of Recommendation

Strong recommendation letters can significantly strengthen your application.

Best practices:

  • Choose recommenders who:
    • Know you well
    • Can speak to your leadership and character
  • Meet with your recommenders to:
    • Explain your goals
    • Share why you are applying
  • Prioritize quality over prestige—a close mentor is better than a distant high-ranking individual

4. Video Introduction (Optional)

This is an opportunity to add a personal dimension to your application.

Tips:

  • Share something new and unique not already in your written application
  • Show personality, passion, and authenticity
  • Highlight:
    • What inspires you
    • What gives you purpose

5. Personal Bio

Your personal bio should function as an elevator pitch.

Include:

  • Academic background
  • Professional experience
  • Leadership achievements
  • Future aspirations

Keep it concise but impactful.

6. Leadership Roles Section

This section is critical for demonstrating impact.

Guidelines:

  • Focus on quality over quantity
  • Clearly describe:
    • Your role
    • Actions taken
    • Measurable impact

Even without formal titles, applicants should highlight leadership through contributions and outcomes.

7. Awards and Recognition

Applicants should:

  • Include awards at all levels:
    • Local
    • Regional
    • National
    • International
  • Explain:
    • Why the award matters
    • How it reflects leadership qualities

APPLY HERE

Top Insights from the Admissions Team

1. Authenticity is the Most Important Factor

The admissions team emphasizes that there is no “perfect” applicant profile. What matters most is:

  • Being genuine
  • Clearly expressing who you are
  • Demonstrating your leadership journey

2. Avoid Common Mistakes

Applicants should:

  • Proofread carefully to eliminate errors
  • Ensure recommendation letters are submitted on time
  • Follow all instructions precisely

3. Plan Your Application Strategically

Recommended approach:

  1. Review the entire application first
  2. Identify time-intensive sections
  3. Contact recommenders early
  4. Work on sections progressively
  5. Leave time for revisions

4. Reapplicants Are Encouraged to Show Growth

Applicants applying again should:

  • Highlight new achievements
  • Demonstrate personal and leadership growth
  • Reflect on how their journey has evolved

5. If You Feel Stuck, Take a Break

Application fatigue is common. The admissions team advises:

  • Taking intentional breaks
  • Seeking feedback from mentors
  • Returning with fresh perspective

Do You Need Experience in China or Global Affairs?

The answer is no.

However, applicants should:

  • Show curiosity about global issues
  • Demonstrate openness to learning about China
  • Explain how the program fits into their leadership journey

Recommended Resources for Applicants

Applicants are encouraged to:

  • Attend application walkthrough sessions
  • Explore the official website
  • Engage with alumni stories on social media
  • Review FAQs and eligibility requirements

For questions or support:

  • Email: admissions@schwarzmanscholars.org

Click HERE to learn more about the Schwarzman Scholars Program.

Final Advice from the Admissions Team

  • Start early and stay organized
  • Be authentic and confident
  • Focus on your unique leadership story
  • Use every section to reinforce your narrative

Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE

The post Schwarzman Scholars 2026 Application Guide: Expert Tips to Build a Winning Leadership Application Before the September Deadline appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Horizon Europe Africa Initiative IV 2026–2027: €605 Million EU Funding Opportunity for African Researchers, Innovators, Universities and Organizations
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The launch of Africa Initiative IV under the Horizon Europe Work Programme 2026–2027 represents one of the most significant international funding opportunities available today for Africa–Europe collaboration in research and innovation. Backed by the European Union, this initiative is designed to strengthen partnerships, fund cutting-edge research, and accelerate solutions to global challenges affecting both continents. […]

The post Horizon Europe Africa Initiative IV 2026–2027: €605 Million EU Funding Opportunity for African Researchers, Innovators, Universities and Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The launch of Africa Initiative IV under the Horizon Europe Work Programme 2026–2027 represents one of the most significant international funding opportunities available today for Africa–Europe collaboration in research and innovation. Backed by the European Union, this initiative is designed to strengthen partnerships, fund cutting-edge research, and accelerate solutions to global challenges affecting both continents.

Overview of Horizon Europe Africa Initiative IV

Africa Initiative IV is part of the broader Horizon Europe Programme, the European Union’s flagship research and innovation funding framework. It builds on previous Africa Initiatives and aligns with the long-term AU–EU Innovation Agenda (2023–2033), which focuses on transforming research into real-world solutions, businesses, and jobs across Africa and Europe.

This initiative was officially launched in 2026 as part of the fourth Horizon Europe work programme (2026–2027), continuing a strong legacy of collaboration between African and European institutions.

Total Funding Value and Scope

  • Total Funding: Approximately €605.45 million
  • Number of Calls: Around 30 calls for proposals
  • Funding Type: Competitive research and innovation grants
  • Submission Platform: EU Funding & Tenders Portal

This makes it one of the largest EU–Africa funding frameworks currently available.

Key Thematic Areas and Research Priorities

Africa Initiative IV focuses on four major priority areas that reflect shared EU–Africa challenges:

1. Public Health

  • Disease prevention and treatment (e.g., HIV, TB, respiratory infections)
  • Health system strengthening
  • Digital health innovations

2. Green Transition

  • Climate change adaptation and mitigation
  • Sustainable energy systems
  • Environmental protection and biodiversity

3. Innovation and Technology

  • Artificial Intelligence and digital transformation
  • Data systems and modeling
  • Advanced materials and critical raw resources

4. Capacity Building in Science

  • Research infrastructure development
  • Skills and institutional strengthening
  • Knowledge transfer and partnerships

These priorities align directly with global development goals and Africa’s innovation needs.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

Africa Initiative IV is designed for collaborative international consortia, not individuals applying alone.

Eligible Applicants Include:

  • Universities and higher education institutions
  • Research institutes and laboratories
  • Startups and innovation hubs
  • Private sector companies
  • NGOs and civil society organizations
  • Government agencies and public institutions

Collaboration Requirement

To qualify, proposals must typically include:

  • At least three independent organizations
  • From different EU Member States or associated countries
  • With African partner institutions included

This ensures strong international cooperation and knowledge exchange.

Eligible Countries and Regions

African Participation

  • Open to all African countries
  • African institutions are strongly encouraged and funded

European Participation

  • EU Member States
  • Horizon Europe associated countries (e.g., UK, Switzerland, others)

This initiative is explicitly designed to foster Africa–Europe partnerships, making cross-continental collaboration mandatory and strategic.

Target Audience: Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for:

  • African researchers and scientists
  • University faculty and academic institutions
  • Innovation-driven startups and SMEs
  • Policy and development organizations
  • International research consortia
  • Technology developers and engineers

It is particularly suited for teams capable of forming strong international partnerships and delivering impactful research outcomes.

How the Application Process Works

Step 1: Identify a Call

Browse available topics aligned with your research or innovation idea.

Step 2: Build a Consortium

Partner with:

  • EU-based institutions
  • African collaborators
  • Industry or policy stakeholders

Step 3: Develop a Proposal

Include:

  • Problem statement
  • Methodology
  • Impact and innovation
  • Budget and timeline

Step 4: Submit Online

Applications must be submitted via the EU Funding & Tenders Portal before the deadline (Brussels time).

Apply here

Why You Should Apply

1. Massive Funding Opportunity

With over €600 million available, this initiative offers substantial financial support for large-scale projects.

2. International Collaboration

Gain access to global expertise, infrastructure, and networks.

3. Real-World Impact

Projects are designed to produce:

  • Tangible innovations
  • Scalable solutions
  • Economic and social benefits

4. Strategic Positioning

Participation enhances:

  • Institutional visibility
  • Research credibility
  • Long-term funding opportunities

Key Benefits for African Participants

  • Direct EU funding access
  • Inclusion in global research networks
  • Capacity building and skills development
  • Technology transfer and innovation scaling

Previous Africa Initiatives have already involved over 40 African countries, demonstrating strong participation and impact.

Important Tips for a Successful Application

  • Start early—consortium building takes time
  • Align your proposal with EU–Africa policy priorities
  • Focus on measurable impact and scalability
  • Leverage existing partnerships and networks
  • Attend webinars and info sessions (e.g., EURAXESS Africa)

Application Deadline (Critical Information)

  • Deadlines vary across calls, ranging from:
    • April 2026 to September 2027

Because deadlines differ by topic, applicants must carefully track specific calls on the EU portal.

The Horizon Europe Africa Initiative IV (2026–2027) is a landmark funding opportunity that goes far beyond traditional grants. It is a strategic platform for transforming African and European research collaboration into tangible solutions that address global challenges. If you are part of an institution, research group, or innovation-driven organization, this is a high-impact opportunity to secure funding, expand partnerships, and contribute to transformative change across continents.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Horizon Europe Africa Initiative IV 2026–2027: €605 Million EU Funding Opportunity for African Researchers, Innovators, Universities and Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Mid-Career Professionals in Canada
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Aga Khan Foundation Canada (AKFC) Global Leadership Program 2026 is a transformative leadership development initiative designed to equip mid-career professionals in Canada with the knowledge, skills, and global perspective required to navigate today’s complex and interconnected world. As global challenges such as climate change, inequality, geopolitical instability, and social fragmentation continue to shape societies, […]

The post Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Mid-Career Professionals in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Aga Khan Foundation Canada (AKFC) Global Leadership Program 2026 is a transformative leadership development initiative designed to equip mid-career professionals in Canada with the knowledge, skills, and global perspective required to navigate today’s complex and interconnected world.

As global challenges such as climate change, inequality, geopolitical instability, and social fragmentation continue to shape societies, there is a growing need for leaders who can think beyond borders and collaborate across sectors. The AKFC Global Leadership Program responds to this need by creating a platform where professionals can deepen their understanding of global issues, enhance their leadership capacity, and contribute meaningfully to building a more inclusive, peaceful, and sustainable future.

Applications are now open for the 2026 cohort, offering a unique opportunity for Canadian professionals to engage in high-level discussions, experiential learning, and global networking.

About the Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program

The Global Leadership Program is a flagship initiative of the Aga Khan Foundation Canada aimed at:

  • Developing globally minded leaders
  • Strengthening leadership capacity across sectors
  • Promoting pluralism, collaboration, and sustainable development
  • Encouraging participants to explore Canada’s role in addressing global challenges

The program brings together a diverse cohort of professionals from different industries and backgrounds, creating a dynamic learning environment where participants exchange ideas, share experiences, and co-create solutions to pressing global issues.

Program Objectives

The AKFC Global Leadership Program is designed to:

  1. Enhance Global Awareness
    • Provide insights into global systems, trends, and challenges
    • Explore the interconnected nature of modern societies
  2. Strengthen Leadership Skills
    • Develop strategic thinking and decision-making abilities
    • Build resilience and adaptability in complex environments
  3. Promote Collaborative Problem-Solving
    • Encourage cross-sectoral dialogue and partnerships
    • Foster collective approaches to global challenges
  4. Empower Purpose-Driven Leadership
    • Help participants align personal values with global impact
    • Inspire action toward building inclusive and sustainable communities

Program Structure and Format

The Global Leadership Program combines virtual learning with an immersive in-person experience, ensuring both flexibility and deep engagement.

1. Virtual Learning Phase (September – October 2026)

Participants will engage in:

  • Peer learning group coaching
  • Interactive virtual sessions
  • Discussions with program alumni
  • Engagement with global thought leaders

This phase allows participants to build foundational knowledge, connect with peers, and prepare for the in-person residency.

2. In-Person Residency in Ottawa (November 1–6, 2026)

A key highlight of the program is the fully funded in-person residency in Ottawa, Canada.

During this residency, participants will:

  • Engage in intensive leadership workshops
  • Participate in simulations (e.g., global policy scenarios such as G20 simulations)
  • Interact with facilitators, guest speakers, and experts
  • Collaborate on real-world global challenges

All costs related to:

  • Travel within Canada
  • Accommodation
  • Meals

are fully covered by the program.

Key Program Themes

Participants will explore a wide range of global issues, including:

  • Climate action and environmental sustainability
  • Global politics and governance
  • Economic development and inequality
  • Social inclusion and pluralism
  • Canada’s role in global affairs

These themes are explored through a combination of lectures, discussions, simulations, and collaborative exercises.

Important Dates

Applicants should take note of the following timeline:

  • Application Opens: April 21, 2026
  • Application Deadline: May 19, 2026 (11:59 PM ET)
  • Shortlisting & Video Submission: Late June – Early July 2026
  • Final Selection Notification: By July 31, 2026
  • Virtual Program Start: September 16, 2026
  • Virtual Sessions: September – October 2026
  • In-Person Residency (Ottawa): November 1–6, 2026

Program Benefits

Participants selected for the AKFC Global Leadership Program will gain access to a wide range of benefits:

1. Fully Funded Experience

  • Travel within Canada covered
  • Accommodation provided
  • Meals included during residency

2. Leadership Development

  • Exposure to global leadership frameworks
  • Training in strategic thinking and problem-solving

3. Networking Opportunities

  • Access to a diverse cohort of professionals
  • Connections with alumni, experts, and global leaders

4. Experiential Learning

  • Participation in simulations and case studies
  • Hands-on engagement with global challenges

5. Long-Term Impact

  • Membership in a growing alumni network
  • Continued collaboration and learning opportunities

Eligibility Criteria

The program is designed for:

  • Mid-career Canadian professionals
  • Individuals from diverse sectors (public, private, non-profit, etc.)
  • Professionals with a demonstrated commitment to leadership and social impact

Applicants should have:

  • Strong interest in global issues
  • Commitment to collaboration and learning
  • Willingness to participate fully in both virtual and in-person components

Testimonials from Past Participants

Participants from previous cohorts have highlighted the program’s transformative impact:

  • It provides clarity of purpose and strengthens leadership direction
  • It offers exposure to diverse perspectives and ideas
  • It builds a strong community of changemakers
  • It inspires actionable insights for real-world challenges

Many alumni describe the program as a defining experience in their leadership journey.

How to Apply

Interested applicants must submit their applications within the official application window.

Application Process Overview:

  1. Submit an online application
  2. Await shortlisting notification
  3. Submit a short video interview response (if shortlisted)
  4. Receive final decision

APPLY HERE

For inquiries and support, applicants can contact:

  • Email: AKFC-GlobalLeadershipProgram@akdn.org

Conclusion

The Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026 offers a powerful opportunity for mid-career professionals to grow as leaders, expand their global perspective, and contribute meaningfully to solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

With a fully funded residency, world-class learning experiences, and access to a strong network of changemakers, this program stands out as one of the most impactful leadership opportunities available in Canada.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline and take a step toward shaping a more inclusive and sustainable future.

Click HERE to learn more about the Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026.

Discover more global Fellowship and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Mid-Career Professionals in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Open Global Power 2026: Fully-Funded Global Energy Leadership Programme in Italy for Emerging Professionals
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The global energy sector is undergoing a profound transformation, driven by the urgent need for sustainability, innovation, and inclusivity. In response to this shift, the Open Global Power (OGP) 2026 Programme, launched by the Enel Foundation, offers a unique and comprehensive opportunity for young professionals to become leaders in the energy transition. This prestigious initiative […]

The post Open Global Power 2026: Fully-Funded Global Energy Leadership Programme in Italy for Emerging Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The global energy sector is undergoing a profound transformation, driven by the urgent need for sustainability, innovation, and inclusivity. In response to this shift, the Open Global Power (OGP) 2026 Programme, launched by the Enel Foundation, offers a unique and comprehensive opportunity for young professionals to become leaders in the energy transition.

This prestigious initiative builds on the success of previous regional programmes and expands into a global platform for advanced training, policy dialogue, and international collaboration. If you are passionate about shaping the future of energy systems, this programme could be a defining step in your career.

Programme Overview

The Open Global Power 2026 programme is designed to:

  • Equip participants with technical, regulatory, and business expertise in sustainable energy
  • Foster a global network of energy professionals and future leaders
  • Promote inclusive, innovation-driven energy transitions across regions
  • Bridge the gap between global best practices and local implementation

Participants will engage in a blended learning journey, combining online coursework with a potential in-person experience in Italy.

Programme Objectives

Open Global Power 2026 has been designed with the following core goals:

  1. Build a global community of current and future energy leaders by connecting professionals from across Africa, Latin America, and beyond
  2. Provide an integrated understanding of energy systems, covering regulation, economics, technology, and geopolitics
  3. Strengthen participants’ capacity to translate global best practices into solutions that are viable in their own local or regional contexts
  4. Foster cross-regional dialogue and peer learning among professionals with diverse professional, cultural, and geographic backgrounds
  5. Promote gender balance, geographic diversity, and merit-based selection throughout every stage of the programme

Programme Structure

The Open Global Power 2026 programme is built around four carefully designed and sequenced modules. Each module serves a distinct purpose in building participants’ knowledge, skills, and professional networks.

1. Foundational Module (Online)

  • Duration: 4 weeks (approx. 6 hours/week)
  • Focus Areas:
    • Energy systems and electricity markets
    • Energy economics and geopolitics
    • Power technologies and innovation
    • Digital tools for energy transition
  • Assessment:
    • Online multiple-choice test (First Assignment)

2. Global Policy and Regulation Module (Online)

  • Duration: ~4 months (June to mid-October)
  • Format: Self-paced + live sessions
  • Key Highlights:
    • Design of energy policies and regulatory frameworks
    • Understanding sustainable energy access strategies
    • Completion of a Country Project (individual or group)
  • Certification Levels:
    • Certificate of Attendance
    • Certificate of Completion
    • Certificate of Excellence

3. Territorial Modules (Online)

  • Duration: 4 weeks (4–8 hours/week)
  • Focus:
    • Regional energy challenges and solutions
    • Infrastructure and regulatory frameworks
    • Socio-economic and environmental considerations
    • Real-world case studies
  • Assessment:
    • Second online multiple-choice test (Second Assignment)

4. Final Residential Module in Italy (In-Person)

  • Duration: Approximately 1 week
  • Eligibility: Top 10% of participants
  • Activities Include:
    • Advanced lectures and workshops
    • Group policy discussions
    • Site visits
    • Networking with global experts

Give Back Opportunity

Graduates may be invited to contribute to future educational initiatives and events organized by the Enel Foundation.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Age: 24–35 years (as of April 13, 2026)
  • Citizenship: African country, Brazil, or Chile
  • Education:
    • Must hold a Master’s, MBA, or PhD
    • At least one degree from Africa, Brazil, or Chile
  • Professional Interest:
    • Demonstrated commitment to a career in energy or electricity sectors
  • Language Requirement:
    • Advanced English proficiency (CEFR C1 level)
  • Mobility:
    • Must be eligible to travel to Italy

Application Process

To apply, candidates must submit the following documents (in English):

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Academic certificates and transcripts
  • Passport copy
  • Motivation letter (max 700 words)
  • Two reference letters

Important Notes:

  • Applications must be submitted via the official BeeZ platform
  • Only one application per candidate is allowed
  • Incomplete applications will not be accepted
  • Once submitted, applications cannot be edited

Selection Process

The selection is highly competitive and merit-based, involving:

  • Academic and professional evaluation
  • Review of motivation and reference letters

Participant Allocation:

  • 100 participants from Africa
  • 60 participants from Brazil
  • 40 participants from Chile

Top-performing participants from the online phase will be selected for the final Italy residency based on:

  • Assignment performance
  • Engagement throughout the programme

Application Deadline

  • Opening Date: April 13, 2026
  • Closing Date: May 15, 2026 (23:59 CEST)

Late applications will not be considered.

Why You Should Apply

Joining Open Global Power 2026 offers:

  • Access to world-class training and academic partnerships
  • Exposure to global energy policies and innovations
  • Opportunity to network with international experts and peers
  • A pathway to becoming a leader in the global energy transition
  • Potential fully-funded experience in Italy

How to Apply

Applicants must apply using the application code:

  • Code: OAPF1397

Click here to learn more and apply

NB. Ensure all documents are prepared and submitted before the deadline to avoid disqualification.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities here

The post Open Global Power 2026: Fully-Funded Global Energy Leadership Programme in Italy for Emerging Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
